<!-- Generated by scripts/sync_chebfun_examples.py. -->
<!-- Source: https://www.chebfun.org/examples/ode-nonlin/FourierCollocationNonLin.html -->

<div class="chebfun-import chebfun-example-import">
<div class='page-header'>
<h1>Nonlinear Periodic ODE</h1>
<h2>Hadrien Montanelli, December 2014 in <a href='../'>ode-nonlin</a><span><a href='/examples/ode-nonlin/FourierCollocationNonLin.m'>download</a><span class='sep-sm'>&middot;</span><a href='//github.com/chebfun/examples/blob/master/ode-nonlin/FourierCollocationNonLin.m'>view on GitHub</a></span></h2>
</div>

<div id='content' class="col-sm-12 example" role="main">
<pre class="mcode-input">LW = 'linewidth'; dom = [0 2*pi];</pre>

<p>Chebfun uses Fourier collocation to solve linear, nonlinear and systems of ODEs with periodic boundary conditions. Consider the nonlinear ODE</p>
<p>$$ u' - u\cos(u) = \cos(4x), $$</p>
<p>on $[0, 2\pi]$, with periodic boundary conditions. For nonlinear ODEs, we need to specify an intial guess; let us try $\cos(x)$. We can solve the ODE in Chebfun as follows.</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">f = chebfun(@(x) cos(4*x), dom);
N = chebop(@(u) diff(u) - u.*cos(u), dom);
N.bc = 'periodic';
N.init = chebfun(@(x) cos(x), dom);
u = N \ f</pre>

<pre class="mcode-output">u =
   chebfun column (1 smooth piece)
       interval       length   endpoint values trig
[       0,     6.3]       89    -0.058   -0.058 
Epslevel = 2.331417e-15.  Vscale = 2.428494e-01.
</pre>

<p>Let us plot the initial guess in dashed blue, and the solution in blue.</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">figure, plot(N.init, '--b', LW, 2)
hold on, plot(u, 'b', LW, 2)</pre>

<p><img src="../../../images/ode-nonlin/FourierCollocationNonLin_01.png" class="figure chebfun-figure" alt=""></p>
<p>The solution $u(x)$ satisfies the ODE to high accuracy:</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">norm(N*u - f, inf)</pre>

<pre class="mcode-output">ans =
     3.379422527647751e-12
</pre>

<p>If we start with another initial guess, we might obtain another solution. Let us try $\sin(x)^2$, plot it in dashed green, and plot the solution in green.</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">N.init = chebfun(@(x) sin(x).^2, dom);
v = N \ f
hold on, plot(N.init, '--g', LW, 2)
hold on, plot(v, 'g', LW, 2)</pre>

<pre class="mcode-output">v =
   chebfun column (1 smooth piece)
       interval       length   endpoint values trig
[       0,     6.3]       81       1.6      1.6 
Epslevel = 1.776357e-15.  Vscale = 1.785953e+00.
</pre>

<p><img src="../../../images/ode-nonlin/FourierCollocationNonLin_02.png" class="figure chebfun-figure" alt=""></p>
<p>The solution $v(x)$ satisfies the ODE to high accuracy too:</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">norm(N*v - f, inf)</pre>

<pre class="mcode-output">ans =
     1.803868478825869e-12
</pre>

<p>For nonlinear ODEs, the relationships between intial guesses and solutions are difficult to analyse. In this example, we chose two different guesses, and this led to two different solutions.</p></div>
        </div>
    </div>
</div>
